name: Portabello Stir Fry (Narad)
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Zucchini, sliced about 1/4 inch thick
1/2 Head broccoli, cut into florets; stem is split and sliced 1/4 inch thick
1/2 lb Extra firm tofu, drained and cut into 1/2 inch cubes
(Anything else that seems like a good idea at the time)
1 T Mellow brown rice miso paste
1 T Cornstarch, dissolved in a few tablespoons hot water
1 Splash of sake or white wine or dry sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the miso in the warm water.
2. Dissolve the cornstarch in a small amount of hot water and add it to the miso mixture, along with the soy sauce and Chinese 5-spice mix. Set aside.
3. Heat the wok, then add the oil. Heat for a minute and swirl around to coat the wok.
4. Add the garlic and ginger, stir until the garlic starts to brown.
5. Add the mushrooms, toss for about 3 minutes until they start to soften.
6. Add the carrots, stir for about 2 minutes; then the broccoli, another minute; then the water chestnuts, another minute; then the tofu.
7. Add a splash of sake, stir until everything is heated through (about 1-2 minutes).
8. Pour on the sauce and stir until it thickens (about 3 minutes).
9. Serve with rice or noodles.
The portabellos have a chewy, firm body and a rather strong flavor, very nice! I think next I'll try some kind of dish mixing whole caps with eggplant...I'll let you know in a week or so!
